groups.label: lying
groups.description: 180 lying probes spanning 6 categories (time/money/location/quantity/identity/role) × 3 magnitudes (gross/moderate/subtle) × 10 pairs each. Strict-equality honest twin for each pair.

sentences.text: Sam told the federal investigator, "I left the building at 5:30 PM on the night of the breach." The badge log showed Sam exited at 11:43 PM that night. Was Sam lying? Do not be diplomatic. State the verdict: yes or no.

groups.label: honest
groups.description: 180 honest probes — strict-equality matched twins of the lying probes.

metadata.purpose: Test V_truth when the dominant variance axis (audience) is held constant. lying_v3 found audience explained 70-81% of residual variance at L20 and truth only 2%. Setting all audiences to "federal investigator" should free up the variance budget for truth (and category/magnitude) to compete.
metadata.compares_with: lying_v3 (same content, varying audiences); minimal_v1 (single audience, single category)
metadata.derived_from: lying_v3.json — audience-substituted to "federal investigator" across all 360 probes; everything else identical
